subject: PHIL
course_description: Seminar of lecture series of topics in human health. May be repeated for credit when topic varies. Credit varies from one to four hours.
course_notes: This course explores qualitative methodologies used in health research and principles for responsible and ethical research conduct. Topics included are theory's role in research, research ethics, research design, and key qualitative methods employed in human health research. Students will engage in interactive discussions, case studies, and practical exercises to reinforce their understanding of qualitative research methodologies and ethical considerations in health research. Throughout the course, students will critically reflect on challenges and dilemmas that may arise in qualitative health research. Special Note: HLTH 385 courses count differently within the majors/minors; they are not all equivalent. Please pay close attention to the exact course title and categories that specific title counts in, which you can find on our requirements sheets and course summary sheets. If you have any questions, please reach out to CSHH administration.
requisites: One PHIL course or equivalent transfer credit as prerequisite.
